zh.Sandhurst, Gloucestershire, England: 英國格盧斯特斯爾桑德爾斯特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda de Beaumont於大約1400年出生在英國戴文維爾時，她的父親William Beaumont34歲，母親Isabel Willington31歲。她於1417年10月16日與Sir Hugh Courtenay結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1467年7月3日在英國戴文哈可姆享年68歲，埋葬在英國戴文艾可謝特。

zh-hans.Sandhurst, Gloucestershire, England: 英国格盧斯特斯爾桑德爾斯特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Matilda de Beaumont于大约1400年出生在英国戴文維爾时，她的父亲William Beaumont34岁，母亲Isabel Willington31岁。她于1417年10月16日与Sir Hugh Courtenay结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1467年7月3日在英国戴文哈可姆享年68岁，埋葬在英国戴文艾可謝特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
English (Kent): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Siegmund.
German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Old German: Mighty battle maid

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda de Beaumont was born about 1400, in Shirwell, Devon, England, her father, William Beaumont, was 34 and her mother, Isabel Willington, was 31. She married Sir Hugh Courtenay on 16 October 1417. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died on 3 July 1467, in Haccombe, Devon, England, at the age of 68, and was buried in Exeter, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda de Beaumont est née environ 1400, à Shirwell, Devon, Angleterre, son père, William Beaumont, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Isabel Willington, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Hugh Courtenay le 16 octobre 1417. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée le 3 juillet 1467, à Haccombe, Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterrée à Exeter, Devon, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Matilda de Beaumont um 1400 in Shirwell, Devon, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Beaumont 34 und ihre Mutter Isabel Willington 31. Sie hat am 16. Oktober 1417 Sir Hugh Courtenay geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb am 3. Juli 1467 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Haccombe, Devon, England und wurde in Exeter, Devon, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda de Beaumont è nata il circa 1400 nel Shirwell, Devon, England, sua padre, William Beaumont, aveva 34 anni e sua madre, Isabel Willington, aveva 31 anni. Lei sposato Sir Hugh Courtenay il 16 ottobre 1417. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì il 3 luglio 1467, nel Haccombe, Devon, England, all'età di 68 anni, e fu sepolta a Exeter, Devon, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Matilda de Beaumontが約1400年にイギリスデボンシュイルウエルで生まれた時，父William Beaumontは34歳，母Isabel Willingtonは31歳であった。1417年10月16日にSir Hugh Courtenayと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1467年7月3日にイギリスデボンハッコインベで68歳で死去し，イギリスデボンエクセターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda de Beaumont(이)가 대략 1400년 영국 데폰 실웰에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Beaumont(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Willington(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1417년 10월 16일 Sir Hugh Courtenay(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1467년 7월 3일 영국 데폰 하c코브에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 데폰 세터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda de Beaumont nasceu aproximadamente 1400, em Shirwell, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Beaumont, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Willington, tinha 31 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Hugh Courtenay em 16 de outubro de 1417. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em 3 de julho de 1467, em Haccombe, Devon, Inglaterra, com 68 anos, e foi sepultada em Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda de Beaumont родилась около 1400 г. в местности ширвелл, девон, Англия, её отцу по имени William Beaumont было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Isabel Willington, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Hugh Courtenay 16 октября 1417 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла 3 июля 1467 г. в местности хаккомб, девон, Англия в возрасте 68 лет и была похоронена в местности эксетир, девон, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Matilda de Beaumont nació aproximadamente 1400, en Shirwell, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, William Beaumont, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Isabel Willington, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Sir Hugh Courtenay el 16 de octubre de 1417. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió el 3 de julio de 1467, en Haccombe, Devon, Inglaterra, a la edad de 68 años, y fue sepultada en Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra.
